

# B21_KEY_MOMENTS

## Key Quotes

**"Your product's not a thermometer, it's a traffic light."**
— Edmund Cuthbert (quoting a founder from his batch)
*Context:* Describing the fundamental insight about talent evaluation — that stack-ranking candidates with precision scores is seductive but wrong. The right model is pass/fail with context, not a leaderboard. This crystallizes the anti-stack-rank philosophy both Careerspan and Superposition share.

**"Every fucking engineer goes down this rabbit hole of like, oh, but is this person a 78 out of 100 and the best?"**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Context:* Visceral frustration with how technical founders approach talent matching. Validates Careerspan's trade-off-space model over ranked lists. Useful for positioning and messaging — shows this isn't just V's opinion, it's a pattern recognized across the industry.

**"No one has latent signals of 'wants to be forward deployed engineer' on the Internet, but they probably would interacting with someone like your product."**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Context:* The moment Edmund articulated Careerspan's core alpha — extracting intent and fit signals from coaching conversations that don't exist anywhere else. This is the clearest external validation of the non-public alpha thesis.

**"Imagine People Data Labs with intent."**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Context:* Describing the API play — Careerspan as infrastructure that delivers enriched candidate data with behavioral intent signals to other recruiting platforms. A powerful one-liner for positioning the B2B API strategy.

**"Trying to contort ourselves into a state that appeals to VCs has effectively led us to run out of runway."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Context:* Candid admission about the cost of VC-shaped storytelling vs. building the actual business. Sets up the strategic pivot toward monetization and acquisition-readiness over fundraising.

**"No recommendation made by our system is not going to be grounded in some explanation that can be latched on to and justified to the employer."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Context:* Articulating Careerspan's explainability advantage — every candidate recommendation comes with reasoning traces. Differentiator against black-box matching systems. Strong for sales messaging.

**"I'm not precious about attribution of where the candidates come from... the customer could just be they all came from you."**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Context:* Offering Careerspan full white-label positioning within a Superposition partnership. Signals deep trust and willingness to let Careerspan own the talent sourcing narrative with shared customers.

**"When I talk to you, I actually believe that there are people that can change this crap system for the better."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Context:* Closing the conversation with genuine conviction about the partnership potential. Reveals V's emotional investment and belief in Edmund as an aligned operator in the talent space.

## Strategic Questions Raised

**"Are you going to monetize on the candidate side or the company side or both?"**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Significance:* The foundational strategic question that opened up the three-path framework (API play, candidate-side agent, niche middle way). Forced clarity on Careerspan's go-to-market direction.

**"What would it actually look like? What's the interaction with the candidate? What does messaging a candidate look like?"**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Significance:* Product-level diligence question that exposed remaining gaps in the candidate communication flow (interstitial pages, email outreach). Surfaced the operational reality behind the vision.

**"Could we... would there be signaling in your product of people who are engineers but more customer-facing or want to be?"**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Significance:* The moment that generated the forward-deployed engineer / GTM engineer niche thesis. Edmund identified the exact sweet spot where Careerspan's non-public alpha is most valuable — roles where paper credentials are insufficient and behavioral signals matter.

**"What part of that deal would you want and what part would we... how would we carve that up?"**
— Edmund Cuthbert
*Significance:* Moved the Marvin Ventures discussion from concept to deal structure in one question. Signals genuine commercial interest, not just advisory curiosity.

*12:30 PM ET — Feb 15, 2026*
